item.title: Inside the lives of the last Jews in Berlin
item.description: A new novel offers a disturbing account of the stark choices faced by Jews in Nazi Germany.

item.title: AI chatbots have a Romance language problem
item.description: English speakers love words that convey confidence. AI noticed.

item.title: Steve Hilton wants to make California golden again
item.description: The former Fox News host sees a path to becoming the first Republican elected governor in 20 years.

item.title: The fertility crisis is also a religion crisis
item.description: Religious communities can offer the social support families need.

item.title: Interstate trade wars are a new front line in red vs. blue
item.description: States should not attempt economic regulation beyond their borders.

item.title: The Enhanced Games was a manifestation of wellness culture gone awry
item.description: Athletes pumping their bodies with chemicals to maximize performance sets a terrible example for everyone else.

item.title: The shell game
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on Iran war negotiations.

item.title: What happens in California after Gavin Newsom?
item.description: With Gavin Newsom's era as California governor ending, could Tom Steyer be the Democrat to step up? Or will skyrocketing costs and crime finally break the party's grip on California? In this episode of "Make It Make Sense," Carine Hajjar, Jason Willick and James Desio explore the future of the Golden State's political landscape, including whether the GOP can capture the governor's mansion and if Spencer Pratt has a real shot at winning the L.A. mayoral race.

item.title: Trump betrayed farmers. Now real signs of anger show.
item.description: Hurt by the war in Iran and war on trade, will farmers still vote red?

item.title: Why the Metro’s RFK Stadium plan is troubling
item.description: The Commanders are returning to Washington. Will D.C. taxpayers foot the bill?

item.title: America’s Ebola policy can be brutal — and backed by data
item.description: Covid proved that home quarantine fails. Ebola demands a harder lesson be learned faster.

item.title: The Montana license plate loophole is a warning
item.description: Enforcement alone won’t fix the growing problem. States should address punitive taxes.
